title: Philosophy Text Study
description: Reading dense philosophical prose slowly enough to change how you think.
summary_pt_br: Leitura de filosofia: um capítulo/semana (até 20p), em voz alta, reescreva, ache a discordância, carregue uma semana.

scenes.subtitle_en: Reading dense philosophical prose slowly enough to change how you think.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Leitura de filosofia: um capítulo/semana (até 20p), em voz alta, reescreva, ache a discordância, carregue uma semana.

scenes.text_en: Reading dense philosophical prose slowly enough to change how you think. Focus: philosophy text study. Twenty pages of philosophy takes a week — that is not slow, it is right.
scenes.text_pt: Leitura de filosofia: um capítulo/semana (até 20p), em voz alta, reescreva, ache a discordância, carregue uma semana. Foco: philosophy text study. 20 páginas de filosofia em uma semana — não é lento, é correto.

scenes.title: Philosophy Text Study
scenes.body_en: Philosophy reading: one chapter per week, max twenty pages. Read aloud. Rewrite each argument in your own words. Find the single claim you disagree with. Carry it a week.
scenes.body_pt: Leitura de filosofia: um capítulo/semana (até 20p), em voz alta, reescreva, ache a discordância, carregue uma semana.

examples.en: Twenty pages of philosophy takes a week — that is not slow, it is right.
examples.pt: 20 páginas de filosofia em uma semana — não é lento, é correto.

examples.en: Rewrite every argument in your own words, or you didn't read it.
examples.pt: Reescreva cada argumento, ou você não leu.

examples.en: Find the one claim you disagree with and sit with it.
examples.pt: Ache a afirmação que discorda e sente-se com ela.

exercise.question: Complete: "Twenty pages of ___ takes a week — that is not slow, it is right."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Twenty pages of philosophy takes a week — that is not slow, it is right."
exercise.hint_pt_br: 20 páginas de filosofia em uma semana — não é lento, é correto.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Rewrite every argument in your own words, or you didn't read it.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Rewrite every argument in your own words, or you didn't read it."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Reescreva cada argumento, ou você não leu.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Philosophy Text Study — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre philosophy text study. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Rewrite each argument in my own words.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What do you do with disagreement?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Find one claim I reject. Carry it a week.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Reading philosophy well changes how you think.

scenes.title: The philosophy circle
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe the four-step philosophy-reading method. Use at least three philosophy terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In his small seminar of working adults, Kumar opened the week with twenty pages of Kant and a cup of black coffee.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Twenty pages of philosophy takes a week — that is not slow, it is simply the correct pace for this kind of prose.

paragraphs.speaker: Learner
paragraphs.en: And we rewrite every single argument in our own words, or we cannot claim to have actually read the passage.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Then find the one claim you disagree with — and carry it with you for the full week before you discuss it aloud.

paragraphs.speaker: Learner
paragraphs.en: Philosophy is thought, not information — and this is the one subject where speed is the enemy of understanding.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Reading philosophy well, Kumar had come to believe, is the only reliable way to slowly change how your own mind works.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Philosophy Text Study". Reading dense philosophical prose slowly enough to change how you think. Twenty pages of philosophy takes a week — that is not slow, it is right. Rewrite every argument in your own words, or you didn't read it. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Philosophy Text Study". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Philosophy Text Study".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Philosophy Text Study".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
